error_signature: Could not connect to your MCP server
title: MCP Client cannot connect to MCP server
symptom: The MCP Client node fails to connect to the MCP server, displaying 'Could not connect to your MCP server'. This occurs consistently when attempting to run workflows that utilize the MCP Client.
applies_when: MCP server is expected to be running
not_this_if: MCP server is not set up
MCP Client node is not configured correctly
root_cause: The error occurs due to gzip compression being enabled on the MCP server, which interferes with the communication between the MCP Client and the server. Disabling gzip allows for proper data transmission and connection establishment.
fix_steps: Access the MCP server settings in your hosting dashboard
agent_instruction: Disable gzip compression in the MCP server settings and save the changes.
